vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for #721: release scripts (release.py / release_publish.py / release_rollback.py / release_e2e.py) cannot find the `gh` CLI on Windows because Python's subprocess.run does not honor PATHEXT for `gh.cmd` -- resolve to an absolute path via shutil.which once and pass that as argv[0], plus env=os.environ.copy() defense-in-depth.

plan.title: Resolve gh absolute path before subprocess.run in release scripts (Windows PATHEXT fix)

narratives.Problem: On Windows, `gh` is installed as `gh.cmd` (PowerShell launcher / Windows shell shim). Python's `subprocess.run(["gh", ...])` does NOT honor PATHEXT when resolving argv[0] via the OS's CreateProcess path, so the launcher cannot be found -- the four release-pipeline scripts (scripts/release.py, scripts/release_publish.py, scripts/release_rollback.py, scripts/release_e2e.py) emit FileNotFoundError or WinError 2 on Windows hosts even when `gh` is on PATH and works fine from the operator's terminal. The release pipeline (#74) and #716 safety hardening companions (publish/rollback/e2e) are platform-incomplete on the only host class where the maintainer runs them.
narratives.Overview: Add a single shared helper `_resolve_gh() -> str | None` to scripts/release.py that returns `shutil.which("gh")`. shutil.which DOES honor PATHEXT, so it returns the absolute path to `gh.cmd` on Windows and `/usr/bin/gh` (or similar) on POSIX. Refactor every gh subprocess invocation in the four release scripts to (1) resolve via the helper, (2) bail with the existing `gh CLI not found on PATH` message when None, (3) pass argv[0] = resolved absolute path, and (4) propagate `env=os.environ.copy()` to `subprocess.run` as defense-in-depth. release_publish / release_rollback / release_e2e re-use the helper via `release._resolve_gh()` since they already `import release`.
narratives.Background: Issue #721 (Windows release-pipeline subprocess PATH bug) is the first defect found post #716's safety-hardening landing (#716 / PR #720). The four release scripts predate the discovery of the PATHEXT subprocess interaction; existing `if shutil.which("gh") is None:` guards correctly probe presence but the subsequent `subprocess.run(["gh", ...])` invocations re-invoke OS path resolution which does not consult PATHEXT. Other gh callers in the repo (issue_ingest.py, reconcile_issues.py, pr_check_protected_issues.py, ...) exhibit the same defect but are out of scope for this PR -- this scope vBRIEF is laser-focused on the four release-pipeline scripts so the fix can land without blast-radius from refactoring unrelated CLI plumbing.
narratives.Constraint: MUST preserve existing exit codes, return tuples, and error messages so test_release.py / test_release_publish.py / test_release_rollback.py / test_release_e2e.py keep passing. MUST NOT change CLI surface (no new flags, no behavioral change on POSIX). MUST NOT widen scope to non-release gh callers in the same PR -- those route to a separate follow-up issue.
narratives.Risk: Existing tests monkeypatch `subprocess.run` with `def fake_run(cmd, **kwargs):` signatures, so adding `env=` propagation is backward compatible. Existing tests monkeypatch `shutil.which` on the module's `shutil` reference, which mutates the actual `shutil` module attribute -- so `release._resolve_gh()` (called from sibling scripts) sees the same patched `which` regardless of which module's `shutil` was monkeypatched. Net risk is low; the fix is mechanical.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) all four release-pipeline scripts resolve `gh` via `release._resolve_gh()` (no remaining bare `["gh", ...]` argv); (2) all four scripts pass `env=os.environ.copy()` to `subprocess.run`; (3) `tests/cli/test_release_subprocess_path.py` covers the helper, the missing-binary fallback, the resolved-path argv[0], the env propagation, and the Windows `gh.cmd` PATHEXT case; (4) existing release-script tests keep passing unchanged; (5) `task check` and `task ci:local` pass.
narratives.Test: Verification: tests/cli/test_release_subprocess_path.py asserts (a) _resolve_gh returns shutil.which result, (b) downstream callers surface 'gh CLI not found on PATH' when which returns None, (c) subprocess.run argv[0] equals the resolved absolute path (NOT bare 'gh') for view_release / gh_release_delete / create_github_release / provision_temp_repo, (d) env= kwarg present and equals os.environ.copy(), (e) when shutil.which returns r'C:\Program Files\GitHub CLI\gh.cmd' the resolved path is used verbatim as argv[0]. Run via `task test` (uv run pytest tests/) and `task check` / `task ci:local`.
narratives.Action: Swarm-scope leaf: independent of any other vBRIEF (touches only release-pipeline scripts + their tests + CHANGELOG); gated only on master. Closes #721 once merged; #716 hardening sweep continues to anchor follow-up release-script defects.

items.title: Add _resolve_gh helper in scripts/release.py and refactor all gh subprocess invocations across the four release scripts to use the resolved absolute path + env=os.environ.copy()

narrative.Action: Add `def _resolve_gh() -> str | None: return shutil.which("gh")` to scripts/release.py. In release.py / release_publish.py / release_rollback.py / release_e2e.py, replace every `if shutil.which("gh") is None:` guard with the helper, and rewrite each `subprocess.run(["gh", ...], ...)` so argv is `[gh_path, ...rest]` and kwargs include `env=os.environ.copy()`. Existing return tuples / exit codes / log messages preserved verbatim.
narrative.Acceptance: `grep -n 'subprocess.run(\["gh"' scripts/release*.py` returns zero matches after the refactor; every `subprocess.run` of a gh command in the four scripts has `env=os.environ.copy()` in its kwargs.

items.title: Create tests/cli/test_release_subprocess_path.py covering _resolve_gh, the missing-binary fallback, resolved-path argv[0], env propagation, and the Windows PATHEXT (gh.cmd) case

narrative.Action: Tests monkeypatch `shutil.which` and `subprocess.run`; capture argv + kwargs; assert argv[0] equals the resolved absolute path and `env=` equals `os.environ.copy()`. Cover at minimum: release_publish.view_release, release_rollback.gh_release_delete, one release.py gh caller (create_github_release), and release_e2e.provision_temp_repo.
narrative.Acceptance: test_release_subprocess_path.py passes alongside the existing four release tests under `task test`; coverage includes the literal `r'C:\Program Files\GitHub CLI\gh.cmd'` PATHEXT case.

items.title: Add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]/Fixed entry and verify .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md checklist

narrative.Action: CHANGELOG entry: 'Resolve `gh` to its absolute path before subprocess invocation in release scripts so Windows PATHEXT shims (`gh.cmd`) work; propagate `env=os.environ.copy()` for defense-in-depth (#721).'
narrative.Acceptance: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]/Fixed contains the entry; PR body references #721 (Refs not Closes -- parent agent closes #721 separately after the rollback test).

x-tracking.scope_authority: deft-721-fix sub-agent dispatch (parent orchestrator's plan stages 1-4)

references.title: Issue #721: release scripts fail on Windows because subprocess.run does not honor PATHEXT for gh.cmd
Issue #74: chore: automate release process and CI changelog enforcement (foundation -- introduced scripts/release.py)
Issue #716: release safety hardening (added scripts/release_publish.py / release_rollback.py / release_e2e.py companion scripts which inherited the same subprocess PATH defect)
